title: U.S. conducts 'self-defense strikes' in Iran as Trump pushes for peace deal
description: Targets included missile launch sites and Iranian boats attempting to emplace mines.

title: Oil prices mixed as U.S. military strikes against Iran cloud Middle East peace prospects
description: Donald Trump also said negotiations with Iran were "proceeding nicely," but cautioned that the U.S. could resume military action if discussions were to collapse.

title: South Korea's Kospi hits new high amid mixed trading in Asia
description: Asia-Pacific markets were set to open broadly higher Tuesday, amid hopes for a breakthrough in the U.S.-Iran peace negotiations.

title: Few Good Options For Central Banks: SocGen's Wei Yao
description: Societe Generale's Global Chief Economist Wei Yao breaks down the heavy pressure facing central banks around the world as they come up against a mix of inflation risk and higher bond yields. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Australian LNG Union Cancels Ichthys Strike as Talks Progress
description: Workers at Inpex Corp.’s Ichthys liquefied natural gas export project in Australia will not strike as planned on Wednesday and Thursday.

title: ASX Drops Most Since 2012 After Revising Up Costs on Tech Revamp
description: ASX Ltd. shares tumbled the most in more than a decade after the embattled exchange operator increased its capital spending guidance as it upgrades critical market infrastructure.

title: Alleged Singaporean Leader of Insider Trading Ring Denied Bail
description: Zhi Ge, the alleged leader of an international insider trading ring, was denied bail by the Singapore High Court.

title: Copper Holds Gain as Traders Track Prospects for US-Iran Deal
description: Copper held a gain as investors monitored progress toward a possible deal to end three months of war in the Middle East.

title: Yuan May Hit Five Per Dollar on Carry-Trade Exit, Macquarie Says
description: China’s onshore currency may strengthen to as much as five yuan per dollar if local firms unwind a massive buildup of greenback holdings, triggering a sharp reversal in capital flows, according to Macquarie Group.

title: China Coking Coal Prices Extend Gains After Shanxi Mine Disaster
description: Chinese coking coal futures pushed higher for a second session as investors watched for a broader government crackdown on the sector after a deadly accident at a mine in Shanxi province.

title: BOJ Deputy Chief Underlines Proper Policy as Key for Bond Yields
description: Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino highlighted the importance of appropriate policy adjustments to keep the faith of market participants amid a recent selloff in government debt.

title: Why China Is Tightening Controls on Overseas Stock Trading
description: China’s efforts to control capital outflows are colliding with growing demand from mainland investors for access to overseas stocks.

title: Treasuries Gain in Catch-Up Trade as Trump Sees Progress on Iran
description: Treasuries rose across the curve after a holiday, as optimism over a potential US-Iran deal pushed oil prices lower and eased inflation concerns.

title: MUFG Is Said to Explore Options for Stake in Indonesia’s Danamon
description: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. is exploring options for PT Bank Danamon Indonesia, including a take-private of the lender or selling some its stake to increase the public float, people with knowledge of the matter said.

title: India Earnings Revival Under Threat as Commodity Prices Surge
description: Even if the Middle East war ends soon, corporate profits would stay under pressure, BofA says.

title: Chinese Chip Stocks Rise in Hong Kong on Hopes for Huawei Tech
description: Chinese semiconductor stocks rose in Hong Kong on optimism over a potential breakthrough in technology by Huawei Technologies Co.

title: Wheat Extends Decline as Traders Digest Iran Ceasefire Talks
description: Wheat futures were on track to extend their longest losing streak since January, as traders returning from a US holiday digested progress in negotiations to end the war in the Middle East.

title: Xiaomi Short Bets Hit Record on Cost Worries Before Earnings
description: Traders pushed bearish bets on Xiaomi Corp. to a record ahead of first-quarter earnings amid concerns over rising memory costs and escalating rivalry in China’s electric vehicle market.

title: Taiwan Overtakes India as World's Fifth-Largest Stock Market
description: Taiwan overtook India in stock market value, powered mainly by a breakneck rally in the world’s largest chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.

title: India to Host Quad Foreign Ministers' Meeting
description: Shruti Pandalai, the India Chair of the Lowy Institute, talks to Bloomberg TV about the upcoming Quad meeting in India, where the security situation in the Strait of Hormuz is expected to be high on the agenda. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Touts Iran Progress Amid Hormuz Strike Reports
description: US and Israeli jets struck a number of Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, local media reported, hours after President Donald Trump had suggested negotiations with Tehran over an interim deal were progressing. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski breaks down what we know about the state of the talks. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US military says it has launched new strikes on southern Iran
description: The strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats were taken in "self-defence", US Central Command says.

title: Social media as bad for young people as smoking, top doctors say
description: The Academy of Medical Royal Colleges says doctors should routinely check on screen time and social media use when seeing younger patients.

title: Iceland, Rattled by Trump’s Greenland Threats, Weighs Joining the E.U.
description: Iceland has stood apart from the rest of Europe. But President Trump’s threats to Greenland have provoked a reconsideration.

title: Inside India’s Manipur State, Where Violence and Division Are Routine
description: Three years after riots tore apart Manipur, the state remains in disarray. Barbed wire and armed checkpoints made it difficult for Times reporters to cross, even before the recent clashes.

title: U.S. Carries Out Renewed Strikes in Southern Iran
description: Military officials said that the strikes targeted missile sites near a major Iranian port that threatened U.S. ships.

title: Netanyahu vows to ‘smite’ Hezbollah with ‘overwhelming’ force
description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vows to ‘smite’ Hezbollah with ‘overwhelming’ force.

title: Istanbul’s Bilgi University reopened after police crackdown
description: Students celebrated after days of protests against the closure decree that would have shut Bilgi University mid-year.

title: Cost of living crisis reshapes Eid spending in Nigeria
description: High costs in Nigeria are reshaping Eid preparations as families adjust spending and cut back on celebrations.

title: Hajj pilgrims arrive near Mecca ahead of Arafah Day
description: Muslim pilgrims have begun arriving in Mina, near Mecca, ahead of the start of Arafah Day.

title: US military launches strikes on southern Iran amid talks in Qatar
description: US Central Command announces 'self-defence' strikes as Tehran's top negotiators gather in Qatar for talks.

title: Canada’s Mark Carney calls treatment of Gaza flotilla activists ‘appalling’
description: The prime minister called for an independent investigation into Israeli mistreatment, but stopped short of penalties.

title: Tensions rise in Bolivia as protesters march toward presidential palace
description: Demonstrators gathered near the presidential palace in La Paz as police maintained blockades and tensions remained high.

title: Iran war live: US strikes Iran’s south, Tehran officials in Qatar for talks
description: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has ordered the restoration of internet services after months of blocked access.

title: China’s student housing, offices top property investment options amid pricing slump
description: Student housing is tipped to be a top choice among investors in mainland China’s property market as demand continues to outstrip the supply for suitable accommodation for university students, according to analysts. Interest in investment in the student housing segment particularly on the mainland, Hong Kong and Australia has risen, according to property consultancy CBRE. “Much of this increase is being driven by mainland China, which has seen a surge in interest this year on the back of steady...

title: A new battlefront for KFC and its Chinese rivals: the lighter meal
description: Fast-food restaurants in mainland China have found a magic bullet for both health and company profit: offering consumers meal options that limit sugar and salt and provide balanced nutrition. Lighter meals are rising in popularity in China, with an estimated consumer base of about 33 million across the country, and are becoming a game-changer in the food and drink sector, with big-name restaurant chains like KFC seeking to capitalise on the rising trend. Yum China, the owner of KFC and Pizza Hut...

title: HSBC Hong Kong targets 7.5 million customers, same number as city’s population
description: HSBC is targeting an increase in its Hong Kong client base to match the city’s population of 7.5 million, a goal to be driven mainly by international investors, according to its Hong Kong CEO Maggie Ng. Hong Kong has already seen almost 2 million new customers sign up to the HSBC Hong Kong office over the past two years, bringing its total customers to 7 million at the end of last year. “It is my short-term target to see the total customers reach 7.5 million. As Hong Kong only has a population...

title: Oil Prices Jump After U.S. Strikes Iranian Missile Sites
description: Oil prices spiked in early Asian trade on Tuesday, after the U.S. military launched strikes on targets in southern Iran, reigniting fears of military action just a day after prices had dropped dramatically on promises of a peace deal. At the time of writing, Brent futures had climbed to $98.39 per barrel, up 2.34% on the session after plunging 7% on Monday. WTI futures also climbed, but, as there was no settlement on Memorial Day, they remained 4.98% down from the start of the week at $91.79. U.S. Central Command confirmed the strikes on Iranian…

title: Oil Could Stay Above $100 for Years, Analysts Warn
description: Oil prices opened trade this week with a decline on reports that a deal between the U.S. and Iran was imminent. Brent crude slipped below $100 for the first time in days. But then President Trump said there was no rush on a deal and the U.S blockade in Hormuz would remain. While traders scratch their heads, analysts are warning that crude could remain well above $100 per barrel for years. This is literally uncharted territory for oil. Negotiations with Iran were “proceeding in an orderly and constructive manner,” the U.S. president…

description: Iranian officials went to Doha on Monday for the negotiations, hours before the U.S. military announced the strikes it said were intended to protect troops. Israel’s leader said his country plans to intensify attacks against Hezbollah, Iran’s ally, in Lebanon.
